---
title: ""
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

```{=html}
<div class="page-header container">
  <p class="page-header-label">Essays</p>
  <h1 class="page-header-title">Writing</h1>
  <p class="page-header-sub">
    On measurement, evidence, and the gap between how decisions
    are meant to be made and how they are made in practice.
  </p>
</div>

<hr style="border-color:#e5e7eb; margin: 2rem 0;">

<div class="essay-grid container">

  <a class="essay-card" href="#">
    <div class="essay-meta">
      <span class="essay-tag">Data Storytelling</span>
      <span class="essay-read">8 min read</span>
    </div>
    <p class="essay-title">What Hip-Hop Taught Me About Construct Validity</p>
    <p class="essay-desc">When you try to measure something as contested as
    lyricism, every methodological flaw in organizational metrics shows up
    in sharp relief.</p>
    <span class="essay-status">Coming soon</span>
  </a>

  <a class="essay-card" href="#">
    <div class="essay-meta">
      <span class="essay-tag">Criminal Justice · Measurement</span>
      <span class="essay-read">10 min read</span>
    </div>
    <p class="essay-title">Counted Wrong: Data, Young Adults, and the
    Criminal Justice System</p>
    <p class="essay-desc">The categories we use to count people in the
    legal system were not designed with young adults in mind — and that
    design failure has consequences.</p>
    <span class="essay-status">Coming soon</span>
  </a>

  <a class="essay-card" href="#">
    <div class="essay-meta">
      <span class="essay-tag">Metrics · Policy</span>
      <span class="essay-read">7 min read</span>
    </div>
    <p class="essay-title">When the Metric Becomes the Policy</p>
    <p class="essay-desc">How measurement decisions quietly become legal
    and organizational ones — and why that matters for anyone who builds
    or uses KPIs.</p>
    <span class="essay-status">Coming soon</span>
  </a>

  <a class="essay-card" href="#">
    <div class="essay-meta">
      <span class="essay-tag">Aging &amp; the Law</span>
      <span class="essay-read">9 min read</span>
    </div>
    <p class="essay-title">The Mercy We Wrote Down and Never Used</p>
    <p class="essay-desc">Compassionate release exists in almost every
    state's statutes and almost nowhere in practice. What a law that's
    present but unused tells us about the difference between writing a
    policy and measuring whether it works.</p>
    <span class="essay-status">Coming soon</span>
  </a>

  <a class="essay-card" href="#">
    <div class="essay-meta">
      <span class="essay-tag">Aging · Measurement</span>
      <span class="essay-read">6 min read</span>
    </div>
    <p class="essay-title">How Old Is Old? The Age the Law Pretends to Know</p>
    <p class="essay-desc">The legal system fixes hard numbers — 18, 50, 65 —
    onto a boundary that no developmental science draws. A short piece on
    operationalizing a category that has no natural boundary.</p>
    <span class="essay-status">Coming soon</span>
  </a>

  <a class="essay-card" href="#">
    <div class="essay-meta">
      <span class="essay-tag">Prediction · Risk</span>
      <span class="essay-read">8 min read</span>
    </div>
    <p class="essay-title">Trust the Checklist: Why Expert Judgment Keeps Losing</p>
    <p class="essay-desc">Decades of evidence say a two-variable rule
    outpredicts the seasoned professional — in pretrial risk, in clinical
    forecasting, almost everywhere we've checked. Why intuition retains its
    hold regardless, and what that preference costs.</p>
    <span class="essay-status">Coming soon</span>
  </a>

  <a class="essay-card" href="#">
    <div class="essay-meta">
      <span class="essay-tag">Data Storytelling · Courts</span>
      <span class="essay-read">7 min read</span>
    </div>
    <p class="essay-title">Cultivating a Court's Data Story</p>
    <p class="essay-desc">Most court data is buried in reports that go
    unread. A field guide for turning administrative numbers into something a
    judge, a funder, or a community will read and remember.</p>
    <span class="essay-status">Coming soon</span>
  </a>

</div>

<p style="text-align: center; padding: 3rem 0 1rem; color: #6b7280; font-size: 0.9rem;">
More essays in progress.
</p>
```
